blocks.heading: The narrative engine: it turns the simulation's raw events into a living story of arcs, memory, news, and identity.

rows.value: The whole narrative pipeline — observing state, multi-tier memory, spawning & resolving story arcs, resolving decision moments, generating prose, assembling news, and building retrospectives.
It makes transient gameplay into a history a player cares about: rivalries, slumps, redemption arcs, franchise reputations that persist across seasons.
It is a pure **observer** — it reads sim/world/brain state and writes only narrative artifacts. It never changes the game; effects are queued and applied by their owners.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "A director watching the sim, casting arcs as the season unfolds."
bigIdea.sub: One orchestrator — `SeasonTick` — runs every day. It expires tags, flushes the stat observer, rolls vignettes, then ticks the **Director**, which polls for eligible templates, **casts** real players into roles, fires beats, and resolves arcs. Memory is written only when a moment passes a **salience** filter.

blocks.title: The pipeline pieces
blocks.intro: A sprawling domain (15+ sub-folders) that groups into a clean pipeline: observe → remember → direct → surface. All of it is driven once per day by `SeasonTick`. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: observer · tags
cards.purpose: Event observation: rolling-window stat tracking with reactive predicates (slump, hot streak), feeding a reference-counted tag layer.

cards.name: memory · persistent
cards.purpose: Two-tier memory: a cheap event log plus salience-filtered rich entries on each Person/Franchise, and persistent grudges (`BeefState`).

cards.name: director · arcs
cards.purpose: The arc engine: spawns, casts, advances, and resolves multi-beat story arcs from a template registry (11 production templates ship today).

cards.name: km · decisions
cards.purpose: Key moments: resolves in-game decision points (clutch shot, fight, goalie pull) via the actor's archetype preferences, optionally surfacing to the user.

cards.name: prose · types-d
cards.purpose: Cache-first prose generation (LLM, disabled by default) plus micro-vignettes for small moments — hat tricks, viral plays, identity band crossings.

cards.name: news
cards.purpose: The user-facing feed: a salience sampler that scores arc beats, resolutions, and key moments and ranks them, with per-team and per-template caps.

cards.name: retrospective · identity
cards.purpose: Long-form synthesis: career & franchise retrospectives (eras, defining moments) and 14 franchise identity tags with band-crossing detection.

blocks.title: The narrative pipeline
blocks.intro: Sim events flow in; the observer emits tags; the Director polls tags to spawn and advance arcs; effects write narrative memory and surface to news and prose.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per stage — what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the pipeline.

components.name: observer · tags
detail: `StatObserver` records each game into rolling 5/10/25-game windows and evaluates reactive predicates — `slump_candidate`, `hot_streak`, goalie hot/cold — buffering tag emissions. On the day boundary `flushTick` applies them in canonical order to the `TagLayer`, a reference-counted store of season- and game-scoped tags, and recomputes prominence for affected people. Tags are the shared currency the Director polls.

files.name: observer/stat-observer.ts
files.desc: Rolling-window stat tracking and buffered emission.

exports.name: StatObserver · TagLayer
exports.desc: The observer and the tag store it feeds.

exports.name: DEFAULT_PREDICATES · computeProminence()
exports.desc: The shipped predicates and prominence calc.

components.name: memory · persistent
detail: A two-tier write model. Every narrative moment appends a cheap `KMEvent` (~150 B) to the league's event log; only moments that pass `isSalient` also write a rich `NarrativeMemoryEntry` (~500–1100 B) onto the relevant Person or Franchise. `persistent/` holds longer-lived state — `BeefState` grudges with intensity that decays daily, pruned on retirement.

files.name: memory/narrative-memory.ts
files.desc: The two-tier write model and query API.

files.name: memory/salience.ts · prune.ts
files.desc: The salience filter and retirement pruning.

files.name: persistent/beef-state.ts
files.desc: Grudge creation, incidents, and daily decay.

components.name: director · arcs
detail: The heart of the domain. `directorTick` runs each day per franchise: it advances active arcs (validate cast → check resolutions → fire beats), then considers new spawns — polling templates by trigger, filtering by cooldowns and the active-arc cap, weighting, and picking one. Casting fills each role from real candidates; if a required role has none, the spawn fails.
An individual arc moves from a mutation-driven `ArcSeed` through casting, beat-firing (each dispatching `Effect`s), to a resolution logged in `arcHistory`.

files.name: director/director.ts
files.desc: The per-day tick: advance arcs, consider spawns.

files.name: director/casting.ts · effects.ts · seeds.ts
files.desc: Role casting, effect dispatch, and the arc-seed queue.

files.name: arcs/registry.ts · arcs/templates/
files.desc: The template registry and 11 production templates.

exports.name: directorTick() · castTemplate()
exports.desc: The daily tick and the role-casting pass.

exports.name: runEffects() · pushArcSeed()
exports.desc: Effect dispatch and mutation-driven spawn queuing.

connections.body: `ArcSeed`s queued by [sim](../domain-sim/domain-sim.html) / [brain](../domain-brain/domain-brain.html) mutations; tags from the [observer](#c-observer)

components.name: km · decisions
detail: `resolveKM` is called from the sim loop with a Key-Moment opportunity (clutch shot, fight invitation, goalie pull, …). It looks up the template, resolves the actor's archetype, and picks the archetype-preferred choice — or, in user mode for a user-controlled actor, surfaces a modal. It returns a `KMOutcome` with the effect for sim to write back.

files.name: km/resolver.ts
files.desc: The resolution entry: archetype pick or user prompt.

files.name: km/registry.ts · types.ts
files.desc: The KM template registry and types.

exports.name: KMOpportunity · KMTemplate
exports.desc: The sim-emitted opportunity and its template shape.

components.name: prose · types-d
detail: `generateProse` is cache-first and disabled by default: it returns a bracketed placeholder unless live mode is on, in which case it calls the Anthropic SDK, tracks cost, and caches the result with a prompt-version stamp. `types-d/` rolls daily micro-vignettes — hat tricks, off-ice incidents, viral moments, identity band crossings — routing each through the salience-aware memory write.

files.name: prose/generator.ts · anthropic-client.ts
files.desc: Cache-first rendering and the live SDK call.

files.name: types-d/roller.ts · registry.ts
files.desc: The daily vignette roller and template set.

exports.name: generateProse()
exports.desc: Cache-first prose with a `ProseTone`; `generateProseLLM` for live mode.

exports.name: rollDailyVignettes() · ALL_TYPE_D_TEMPLATES
exports.desc: The vignette pass and its 7 templates.

components.name: news
detail: `assembleFeed` gathers candidates (arc beats, resolutions, key moments) and scores each with a multi-factor `SalienceBreakdown` — surface level, team and subject prominence, freshness, scope, and proximity to the user's team — then ranks them, enforcing per-team and per-template caps. `backfillTeamPage` tops up a quiet team's feed.

files.name: news/sampler.ts
files.desc: Candidate gathering and the ranked feed.

exports.name: assembleFeed() · scoreCandidate()
exports.desc: The feed builder and per-candidate scorer.

exports.name: NewsItem · SalienceBreakdown
exports.desc: The ranked item and its score components.

connections.body: the `app/` UI as the player-facing feed

components.name: retrospective · identity
detail: `generateCareerRetrospective` is cache-first: it chunks a person's narrative into eras, selects era-balanced defining moments, and (in live mode) prose-renders a lede and legacy. `identity/` defines the 14 franchise identity tags (spender, developer, meddler, winning-culture, playoff-pedigree, …) with band boundaries and antagonism pairs; arc/KM effects accumulate per-season contributions that the world's identity tick applies.

files.name: identity/tags.ts
files.desc: The 14 tag definitions, bands, and antagonism pairs.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Narrative artifacts are written onto the world's entities (Person/Franchise/League) but defined and governed here.

rows.name: Arc · ArcTemplate · ArcResolution
rows.desc: Active story arcs, their blueprints (roles, beats, resolutions), and the log of how each resolved.

rows.name: KMEvent · NarrativeMemoryEntry
rows.desc: The cheap event log and the rich, salience-filtered memory entries on each Person/Franchise.

rows.name: BeefState
rows.desc: Persistent grudges between people (0–100 intensity), e.g. a player traded against their will.

rows.name: identityTags (14 tags)
rows.desc: Per-franchise reputation: spender/frugal, developer, meddler, winning-culture, playoff pedigree…

rows.name: CachedRetrospective
rows.desc: Versioned, cache-first career/franchise summaries (eras + defining moments).

blocks.note: Reads (never mutates): the season-event bus & stats from `lib/sim`; entities from `lib/world`; archetype & arc-decision logic from `lib/brain`.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: One orchestrator in, narrative artifacts out.
blocks.note: Subscribes to `sim`'s season-event bus and reads its stats; reads `world` entities; calls `brain` for archetype-driven decision resolution. All one-way — nothing downstream depends on `story`.
